ONE SANSOME STREET, SUITE 1000
TELEPHONE (415) 981-4415
FAX (415) 421-2727

VENTURA, CA 93003
674 COUNTY SQUARE DR., SUITE 2030
TELEPHONE (805) 658-5436
FAX (805) 658-6804

IN REPLY REFER
TO OUR FILE NO.

BAKERSFIELD
42911-20390

California Political Fair
Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Ste. 600
Sacramento, CA 95818

TO WHOM IT MAY CONCERN:

We represent a non-profit mutual benefit corporation
which has several organizations as members. Some of the
members are public entities while others are private entities.

One of the private members wishes to donate office
space to the non-profit mutual benefit corporation with the
corporation only paying for the costs to operate the office
space.

Some of the public organizational members expressed
concerns suggesting that such an arrangement might constitute
an illegal political contribution.

We have performed extensive legal research on this
issue and have found no legal authorities or sources breaching
this subject.

On June 19, 1990, we contacted an official in your
office named Kevin Braaten-moen. We discussed the above
subject with Mr. Braaten-moen and he assured us that such an
arrangement did not constitute an illegal political contribution since there was no political candidate or campaign involved
in the situation.

BORTON, PETRINI & CONRON


California Political Fair
Practices Commission
June 20, 1990
Page Two


     We asked him to send a conformation letter to us outlining
his opinion.  However, he instructed us to write your office
first and that someone would respond to our issue in writing
later.

     If you should have any questions concerning this matter,
please contact us at telephone number (805) 322-3051.  Thank you
for your time and consideration to this matter.

                                        Yours truly,

                                        [signature]
                                        CHRISTIAN G. HOWLAND

CGH:jp
cc : Mike Ammann, President
     Kern Economic Development Corp.

embedding.qa_text: The letter is from the Law Offices of Borton, Petrini & Conron and addresses the California Political Fair Practices Commission regarding a non-profit mutual benefit corporation that has several organizations as members, some public entities and some private entities. One of the private members wishes to donate office space to the non-profit mutual benefit corporation with the corporation only paying for the costs to operate the office space. Some of the public organizational members expressed concerns suggesting that such an arrangement might constitute an illegal political contribution. The law firm has performed extensive legal research on this issue and found no legal authorities or sources breaching this subject. On June 19, 1990, they contacted an official in your office named Kevin Braaten-moen. They discussed the above subject with Mr. Braaten-moen and he assured them that such an arrangement did not constitute an illegal political contribution since there was no political candidate or campaign involved in the situation. BORTON, PETRINI & CONRON California Political Fair Practices Commission June 20, 1990 Page Two We asked him to send a conformation letter to us outlining his opinion. However, he instructed us to write your office first and that someone would respond to our issue in writing later. If you should have any questions concerning this matter, please contact us at telephone number (805) 322-3051. Thank you for your time and consideration to this matter. Yours truly, [signature] CHRISTIAN G. HOWLAND CGH:jp cc: Mike Ammann, President Kern Economic Development Corp.
